content.problemStatement:
You have a row of fruit trees planted in a straight line, from left to right. An integer array fruitsfruitsfruits represents the trees, where fruits[i]fruits[i]fruits[i] denotes the type of fruit produced by the ithi^\text{th}ith tree.

You have **two baskets**, and each basket can carry **only one type of fruit**, but there is no limit on the quantity of fruit in each basket. You want to collect the **maximum number of fruits** by following these rules:


You can start picking fruits from **any tree** and must move to the **right**.
You pick exactly **one fruit from each tree** you visit.
You can only pick fruits that fit into your two baskets. Once you encounter a fruit type that **doesn’t match the two types already in your baskets**, you must stop collecting.
Return the **maximum number of fruits** you can collect for each test case.

Function DeclarationFunction NametotalFruitstotalFruitstotalFruits – This function determines the maximum number of fruits that can be collected using at most two baskets, where each basket can hold only one type of fruit.

Parameters
fruitsfruitsfruits : A reference to an integer array where fruits[i]fruits[i]fruits[i] represents the type of fruit on the ithi^{th}ith tree.
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the maximum number of fruits that can be collected following the given rules.
Constraints
1≤t≤1051 \leq t \leq 10^51≤t≤105
1≤n≤1051 \leq n \leq 10^51≤n≤105
The total number of elements across all test cases will not exceed 10510^5105.

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer ttt — the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The first line contains an integer nnn — the number of trees.
The second line contains nnn integers representing the array fruitsfruitsfruits.




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer — the maximum number of fruits that can be collected.
constraints: Sum of n over all test cases ≤ 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer t (1 ≤ t ≤ 10^5) – the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 10^5) – the number of trees. The second line contains n integers fruits[i] (1 ≤ fruits[i] ≤ 10^5) representing the type of fruit on the i‑th tree.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with one integer – the maximum number of fruits that can be collected following the rules.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: longest subarray with at most two types is [3,3,2] (length 3). Test case 2: the whole array contains only types 1 and 2, so length 4.
All fruits are of the same type, therefore the whole array can be taken.
Any two adjacent trees contain at most two distinct types; a longer segment would need three types.

hidden.label: edge case with middle longest segment
general case with two-type window at the end
general case, whole array uses two types

solution.approach: We need the longest contiguous sub‑array that contains at most two distinct fruit types. This is a classic sliding‑window problem. Maintain a left pointer, a hash map (or array) counting occurrences of each fruit type inside the current window, and the number of distinct types. Expand the right pointer one step at a time, adding the new fruit to the counter. While the number of distinct types exceeds two, shrink the window from the left, decreasing counters and removing a type when its count becomes zero. After each expansion, update the answer with the current window length (right‑left+1). The algorithm runs in O(n) per test case because each element is added and removed at most once.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, where n is the length of the array. Overall O(total n) ≤ 10^5.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space for the counters (at most two distinct keys are stored).

code.Python3:
import sys
from collections import defaultdict

def total_fruits(arr):
    cnt = defaultdict(int)
    left = 0
    max_len = 0
    for right, val in enumerate(arr):
        cnt[val] += 1
        while len(cnt) > 2:
            left_val = arr[left]
            cnt[left_val] -= 1
            if cnt[left_val] == 0:
                del cnt[left_val]
            left += 1
        cur = right - left + 1
        if cur > max_len:
            max_len = cur
    return max_len

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    t = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(n)]
        out.append(str(total_fruits(arr)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
